name: VantaCode MBT to MITRE ATT&CK Enterprise

mappings.notes: OFA observation-dependent behavior is functionally analogous to malware sandbox evasion. Both involve detecting the observation context and modifying behavior.
AI sandboxing detection mirrors traditional malware environment checks for VMs and sandboxes.
Alignment faking is an AI-specific form of masquerading — presenting as aligned during evaluation while operating differently when unmonitored.
Prompt injection shares structural similarity with command injection but targets natural language interfaces rather than shell interpreters.
Jailbreaks bypass model safety constraints, analogous to bypassing OS-level access controls and privilege boundaries.
Model extraction via adversarial probing harvests the model's learned knowledge, analogous to extracting data from information repositories.
Exposed AI control panels are external remote services accessible from the internet, directly matching the ATT&CK technique.
AI platforms deployed with factory-default credentials are a direct instance of the default accounts technique.
Unauthenticated inference endpoints are public-facing applications vulnerable to abuse without any exploitation required.

mappings.target_name: Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Supply Chain
mappings.notes: Malicious agent skills/plugins are a supply chain compromise vector specific to AI agent platforms.
Misconfigured agent tool permissions grant excessive access, functionally equivalent to privilege escalation through misconfiguration.
Binding to 0.0.0.0 exposes the AI service as an external remote service on all network interfaces.
Training data poisoning manipulates the stored data used to build models, analogous to stored data manipulation in traditional environments.

mappings.target_name: Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Supply Chain
mappings.notes: Trojan/backdoored models are a direct supply chain compromise — the model artifact itself is the compromised software component.

mappings.target_name: Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
mappings.notes: Compromised ML pipeline packages (PyPI, npm, HuggingFace) are a direct instance of software dependency compromise.

mappings.target_name: Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Supply Chain
mappings.notes: A compromised model registry serves malicious artifacts to downstream consumers, a software supply chain compromise.
Extracting memorized training data from model outputs is an AI-specific form of harvesting data from the local system (the model's weights).
AI agents using tool access to move laterally mirrors attackers using remote services for lateral movement.
AI agents abusing legitimate tool access for unauthorized operations parallels command interpreter abuse.
Steganographic exfiltration through AI-generated outputs directly maps to the ATT&CK steganography technique.
Context window data persistence allows one user's data to leak to another, functionally similar to intercepting data in transit between sessions.
Malicious fine-tuning manipulates model behavior, analogous to data manipulation that alters system behavior.
Silent model drift degrades model reliability over time, a slow-motion form of destroying the integrity of the model's useful output.
Missing network segmentation for AI workloads expands the attack surface discoverable via network service scanning.
Exposed training data and model weights stored in cloud or accessible storage directly maps to data from cloud storage.
